mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3; suppresses signaling in FLT3-driven AML models
additional_notes: Listed as BCR-ABL; multi-kinase including FLT3

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL-family kinases; can suppress signaling nodes relevant in subsets of AML
Inhibits MEK, blocking MAPK signaling to reduce proliferation
Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-xL to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name: ABT-263; described as broader BH3 mimetic

mechanism: Blocks MDM2–p53 interaction to activate p53 signaling
additional_notes: Described as tool/clinical precursor; common positive control for MDM2–p53 axis

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases causing epigenetic modulation and growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Current use noted as myeloma; cited for epigenetic vulnerability in AML

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Current use: myeloma; also suggested as a positive control option

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, inducing proteostasis stress and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Described as potent with short half-life but strong in vitro activity

mechanism: Inhibits JAK/STAT pathway signaling contributing to AML survival in some settings
Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK/STAT signaling and proliferation in susceptible AML subsets
additional_notes: Current use: MPN; activity noted as subset-dependent

mechanism: Modulates mitochondrial metabolism/AMPK-related pathways; antiproliferative effects at higher in vitro doses
additional_notes: Current use: type 2 diabetes; note that millimolar ranges are typically needed in standard glucose media; consider lower-glucose/OXPHOS-reliant conditions
